title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox achieve an 11-game win streak after sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Jarren Duran and Carlos Narvaez contribute significantly to the Red Sox's offensive performance in Game 1.
Boston improves to a .500 record and occupies the final AL wild-card spot after the doubleheader sweep.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are progressing well in their recoveries and could return soon.
Kutter Crawford is throwing from 100 feet, Tanner Houck is throwing off a mound, and Johan Oviedo will have a bullpen session.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others remain sidelined with no updates on their injury recoveries.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run, had an RBI, and three hits, boosting his role as Red Sox's designated hitter.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 with an RBI and two runs, consistently applying pressure on the Rays' pitchers.
Pitcher Jake Bennett threw 6 scoreless innings, giving up one hit and ensuring bullpen resources for Game 2.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft expressed support for Patriots coach Mike Vrabel despite his offseason controversy involving NFL reporter Dianna Russini.
Vrabel denied affair allegations with Russini after being photographed together; he missed NFL draft's third day for family discussions.
Russini resigned from The Athletic following an investigation; she stated that the photos taken did not reflect the entire context.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky,' a crime series now streaming on Apple TV.
'Obsession,' a 2026 horror film, grossed over $400 million and is available on Peacock.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople,' featuring Sam Neill, is streaming on Prime Video and Plex.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin was found guilty of violating NH Civil Rights Act in a 2024 assault on a transgender woman.
The court ordered Lufkin to avoid the victim and imposed a $5,000 civil fine for the bias-motivated attack.
Lufkin pleaded guilty to assault charges, receiving a 12-month sentence with most suspended for three years.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox won 10 games straight, placing them as potential American League contenders despite early season struggles.
ESPN's Jeff Passan states Boston's plans for the Aug. 3 trade deadline are 'fluid' despite recent success.
MLB insider Jon Morosi reports trading All-Star closer Aroldis Chapman is increasingly unlikely due to improved playoff odds.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket, owned by Evan and Maria Marley, is for sale at $12.5 million.
The sale includes the restaurant at 11 West Creek Road and staff housing at 41.5 Nobadeer Farm Road.
Evan Marley plans to assist in the transition after the sale to ensure continuity of the business.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: The French national soccer team departed Boston for Miami after staying since early June at the Four Seasons Hotel.
France will play England in Miami for third place after their 2-0 loss to Spain in the World Cup semifinals.
While in Boston, the team trained at Bentley University in Waltham and rented out the entire hotel for their stay.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon's production company, Artists Equity, is developing a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of murder charges in the 2022 death of Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe.
Artists Equity joins multiple production companies adapting the controversial case, including Lifetime and Prime Video.
